definition: an accountant is someone whose job is to organise the financial records for a company or for a person
example: he’s an accountant for a big law firm

definition: accounts are the financial records of a person or organisation
example: I’m in the accounts department
he wants to study accountancy

definition: a labourer is someone whose job involves using physical strength
example: she was the daughter of a farm labourer from Sussex

definition: a plumber is someone whose job is to repair and connect water pipes, sinks, baths, toilets etc
example: a good plumber can earn a lot of money
check the plumbing for leaks and loose pipes

definition: something that is repetitive happens again and again, often in a very boring way
example: working in the factory involved doing a series of repetitive tasks
my music practice involves repeating the same exercise several times

definition: a surgeon is a doctor who cuts people’s bodies open in order to repair them or replace parts of them
example: it takes years of training to become a surgeon

definition: trade is the business of buying and selling or exchanging things. A trader is someone who buys and sells or exchanges goods
example: her great-grandfather was a fur trader in Canada
the company does a lot of trade with China
the company does a lot of trade with China
it was a major trading centre for wool

definition: a truck is a very large road vehicle that is used for transporting goods. In British English, the word lorry is often used instead of truck
example: the truck was carrying a load of bricks

definition: admin is all the tasks involved in organising the management of an organisation. The word admin is short for administration
example: the job mostly involves admin and paperwork

definition: a bonus is extra money you get from your employer because you have worked hard or done well
example: he gets a big bonus every year on top of his salary

definition: if an activity is competitive, everyone doing it is trying hard to do better and be more successful than everyone else
example: advertising is a highly competitive industry
small companies find it hard to compete in the market
there’s a lot of competition for jobs

definition: someone’s living or working conditions are all the things that affect the way they are able to live or work
example: working conditions in the factory were unacceptable

definition: a deadline is the time or date by which you have to finish a particular piece of work
example: I have a deadline to meet by Friday

definition: delivery is the act of taking things such as goods or letters to a place
example: my job was to prepare all the orders for delivery
we can deliver direct to your door

definition: a situation or person that is demanding is difficult to deal with because they need a lot of your time or energy
example: it’s a physically demanding job
the lessons will demand all your attention
the lessons will demand all your attention

definition: something that is dull is not very interesting
example: the job’s a bit dull but the pay is good

definition: a field is a particular kind of activity
example: lots of people want to get into that field

definition: a fortune is a large amount of money
example: she must be earning a fortune

definition: if you are in charge, you control something or are responsible for it
example: I’m in charge of 30 staff

definition: if the situation you are in is insecure, it is not safe or protected and might change to a worse situation
example: having a short contract makes you feel very insecure
financial insecurity
he found a secure job in the local factory
nobody has job security any more

definition: the opposite of a word is a different word whose meaning is as different as possible. For example, the opposite of “up” is “down”, and the opposite of “happy” is “sad”
example: what’s the opposite of “old”?

definition: an order is a request to make, supply, or deliver something
example: we need to complete this order by Saturday (get it ready to deliver to someone)
I’d like to order a taxi to the station

definition: when a company recruits employees, it finds people to come and work for it
example: my job was to recruit new programmers

definition: recruitment is the business and activity of finding people to come and start working for an organisation or company
example: I’m the manager responsible for recruitment

definition: if someone or something is respected, people like and admire them
example: a well-respected law firm
if you behave badly, you’ll lose everyone’s respect
if you behave badly, you’ll lose everyone’s respect

definition: your responsibilities are the things you have to deal with, especially when they are officially part of your job
example: it’s an easy job with no real responsibility
he is responsible for packing all the orders

definition: if something is rewarding, you get pleasure, money, or satisfaction by doing it job/career
example: it’s a very rewarding job
he got the rewards for all his hard work
he got the rewards for all his hard work

definition: if you are self-employed, you work for yourself and not as an employee of a company
example: being self-employed can be quite insecure
after three years of self-employment I went back to working for a company

definition: if something is temporary, it only lasts for a limited period of time
example: a temporary job
several roads were temporarily closed during the storms
he’s looking for a permanent job
his knee was permanently damaged in the accident

definition: something that is vague is not clear or definite
example: he made some vague promises
I can only vaguely remember my first day at school

definition: if something is varied, it changes a lot or it consists of lots of different kinds of things
example: it’s a very varied job
prices vary quite a lot
they offer a wide variety of courses

definition: a code is a set of rules that say what you are allowed to do
example: is there a dress code for the party? (rules about what sort of clothes you are allowed to wear)

definition: corruption is dishonest and illegal behaviour by people who are in a position of power or authority
example: they’re trying to reduce corruption
there are at least a dozen corrupt officials in the department

definition: a crisis is a sudden and very serious problem that needs to be dealt with straight away
example: there’s a crisis at the office

definition: discrimination is deliberately unfair treatment of a person or group of people
example: discrimination still happens, even though it’s against the law
critics say the new laws discriminate against women
discriminatory policies that gave benefits to the white residents

definition: employment is the state of having a job or of employing someone
example: the government introduced new employment laws
the company employs over 2,000 workers
the company employs over 2,000 workers
the company employs over 2,000 workers

definition: to enforce a rule or law means to use your authority to make sure that it is obeyed
example: the government doesn’t do enough to enforce the law
law enforcement is a priority here

definition: environmental means relating to the protection of the air, land, and natural water on the earth
example: the company was found guilty of breaking environmental laws
stronger laws are needed to protect the environment

definition: an exception is a person or thing that is different from others. If you make an exception, you deliberately do something that is different from what is meant to happen
example: they made an exception in his case
collocations: with the exception of someone or something

example: it’s an exceptional school (different from others because it is extremely good)
an exceptionally gifted child (one who is much more clever than most other children)

definition: to expose someone to something means to make it possible that something harmful or dangerous will happen to them
example: you mustn’t expose them to danger
prolonged exposure to sunlight can cause skin cancer

definition: if a person or organisation is fined, they have to pay money as a punishment for something wrong that they have done
example: the company was fined because it had broken health and safety rules
she was ordered to pay a fine of £250

definition: if your employer fires you, they officially tell you that you no longer have a job
example: it is now easier for companies to hire and fire people

definition: a gift is a present that you give to someone
example: he offered me a generous gift of £100

definition: health and safety is the set of rules that have to be obeyed by employers to make sure that employees and customers are safe in offices, shops, factories etc
example: the company had broken health and safety rules

definition: if you do something in advance, you do it before a particular date, or before it is needed
example: you’re supposed to make the arrangement a month in advance

definition: if a virus infects someone, it makes them ill. If a virus infects a computer, it destroys or damages the information on the computer
example: if you click on that link, you’ll end up on a site that will infect your PC
a powerful infection that spread across the whole network

definition: if you interrupt someone, you get their attention while they are busy doing something else or speaking to someone else
example: I’m sorry to interrupt, but can I have a quick word?
there were a few brief interruptions during the speech

definition: if you give someone notice about something, you tell them about it some time before it happens
example: I’m sorry for the short notice, but we need it today

definition: if you are off, or if you take time off, you are not at work because you are ill or having a holiday
example: I need to take the day off on Friday

definition: property is land and buildings
example: you mustn’t smoke on any part of the company property

definition: a requirement is something that must happen or be done because of a law or rule
example: it’s a legal requirement to pay at least the minimum wage
the law requires you to register as self-employed

definition: risk is the possibility that an action might cause harm if it does not work out properly. If you put someone at risk, you put them in a situation where there is a possibility that something bad might happen to them
example: they had put their employees at risk
if you go too close, you risk catching the disease
it’s risky to invest all your money in one company

definition: if something is sensible, it seems good because there are good reasons for it and people agree that it is right
example: do you think the rules are sensible and fair?
we need to discuss the problem sensibly

definition: a system is all the related parts of a process or machine such as a computer network
example: the computer virus infected the whole system

definition: a virus is a small living thing that can cause disease in humans or animals. If a computer gets a virus, it has software on it that deliberately tries to harm it
example: his laptop got a virus
a viral video (a video on the Internet that is suddenly very popular and is watched by a large number of people)

definition: a warning is a statement telling someone about something bad that might happen to them. If an employee is given a warning, he or she is told that they must improve their work or they will face a punishment
example: he’s been given a written warning
I warned you not to do that
collocations: warn someone not to do something

definition: abuse is deliberately rude and insulting language
example: we get quite a lot of abuse from customers

definition: if you bend something, you make it folded and not straight
example: a strong metal that won’t bend easily
there’s a sharp bend in the road
the wheel got bent out of shape

definition: if something benefits you, it gives you something good or gives you an advantage
example: will anyone benefit financially from the project?
one of the benefits of the climate here is that heating bills are low

definition: boredom is the state of being bored, when you are not at all interested in what is happening or what you are doing
example: I thought I’d die of boredom
I was bored stiff (extremely bored)
am I boring you?
I was bored stiff (extremely bored)

definition: if you are grateful to someone, you are glad that they have helped you or given you something
example: the families are very grateful for the job I’ve done
all suggestions will be gratefully received
I can’t express my gratitude enough

definition: if you handle something, you touch it or move it with your hands
example: you shouldn’t handle rotting meat without gloves on

definition: your motivation is the reason why you do something
example: he has no motivation to study
their teacher really knows how to motivate them
the motive behind the attack is unclear
his success motivated the rest of the students to do better
greed was a motivating factor in his decision

definition: if something is nasty, it is bad and unpleasant
example: technology could solve some nasty problems

definition: if you predict what will happen, you say what you think will happen in the future
example: experts predict that the situation is going to get worse
his prediction of the election result was surprisingly accurate
the ending of the film was very predictable (you could predict what would happen)

definition: if you preserve something, you keep it fresh, or stop it from rotting or being destroyed
example: chemicals that are used to preserve dead bodies
preservation of the environment

definition: you use relatively when you are making comparisons. For example, if something is relatively good, it is good when compared to similar things. If someone is relatively tall, they are tall compared to other people
example: it’s a relatively quick way of earning money
a period of relative peacefulness

definition: when plants or dead animals rot, they become softer, start to smell, and eventually become destroyed in a natural process. Rotting substances are going through this process
example: the smell of rotting meat
the meat was so old it had started to rot
the smell of rotten eggs

definition: side effects are unpleasant and unplanned effects of a medicine
example: one serious side effect is a loss of vision

definition: if you slam something, you hit it hard against a hard surface
example: he slammed down the phone

definition: if you stare at something, you look at it for a long time
example: they have to stare at a computer all day
